content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 23, 1993

Michael Kenny
General Counsel
Air Resources Board
2020 L Street
Sacramento, Ca 95812

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-93-087

Dear Mr. Kenny:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
duties of members of the Rice Straw Burning Alternatives Committee
(RSBAC) under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTIONS

1. Are advisory committee members of the RSBAC public
officials making or participating in making governmental
decisions? If so, what are their reporting responsibilities under
the Act.

2. If RSBAC members are public officials under the Act,
under what circumstances, if any, may the RSBAC members, in
particular the two who are rice growers, make or participate in
making governmental decisions?

3. If there are no circumstances under which certain RSBAC
members may make or participate in making governmental decisions,

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No.
A-93-087
Page 2


how can the Health and Safety Code section 41865 and Government
Code section 87100 be harmonized?

CONCLUSIONS

1.     The RSBAC appears to be solely advisory in nature and,
therefore, the members need not be designated in a conflict of
interest code and are not subject to the Act's conflict of
interest provisions.

     2. and 3. Based on the answer to the first question, it is
not necessary to answer these questions.

FACTS

     Health and Safety Code Section 41865 requires the Air
Resources Board (ARB) and the Department of Food and Agriculture
(DF&A) to establish an advisory committee to assist with the
identification and implementation of alternatives to rice straw
burning. Members of the committee must be from the Sacramento
Valley Air Basin, and the committee must consist of two rice
growers, two representatives from the environmental community, two
health officials, two county supervisors or their designees, one
member from the air pollution control council, and one member from
the business community with expertise in market or product
development. The committee must meet at least annually. General
Fund moneys may not be used to support the committee.

     The advisory committee is required to develop a list of
priority goals for the development of alternative uses of rice
straw for the purpose of developing feasible and cost-effective
alternatives to rice straw burning. These goals must include, but
not be limited to, research on alternatives, economic incentives
to encourage alternative uses, and new product development.

     On or before September 1, 1995, the ARB and FD&A must jointly
report to the Legislature on the progress of the phasedown of rice
straw burning. The report will include an economic and
environmental assessment, the status of feasible and cost-
effective alternatives to rice straw burning, any recommended
changes to Health and Safety Code Section 41865, and other issues
related to that section. The report must be updated biennially
and transmitted to the Legislature not later than September 1,
1997, 1999, and 2001, respectively.

     The ARB and DF&A issued appointment letters to the 10
advisory committee members of the RSBAC on January 27, 1993.

ANALYSIS

     Government Code Section 87300 requires every agency to adopt
and promulgate a conflict of interest code. A conflict of
interest code is a document which specifically enumerates each of
the positions within the agency which involve the making or

Our File No.
A-93-087
Page 3


participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably
have a material effect on any financial interest and for each such
enumerated position, the specific types of investments, business
positions, interests in real property, and sources of income which
are reportable.  An investment, business position, interest in
real property, or source of income must be reported by the
conflict of interest code if the business entity in which the
investment or business position is held, the interest in real
property, or the income or source of income may foreseeably be
affected materially by any decision made or participated in by the
designated employee by virtue of his or her position.  (Section
87302).

     The term "designated employee" means any officer, employee,
member or consultant of any agency whose position with the agency
is designated in a conflict of interest code because the position
entails the making or participation in the making of decisions
which may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial
interest.  The term "designated employee" does not include any
unsalaried member of any board or commission which serves a solely
advisory function.  (Section 82019.)

     "Member" includes any salaried or unsalaried member of a
board of commission with decision-making authority.  A board or
commission possesses decision-making authority whenever:

     (A)  It may make a final governmental decision;

     (B)  It may compel a governmental decision; or may prevent a
          governmental decision either by reason of an exclusive
          power to initiate the decision or by reason of a veto
          which may not be overridden; or

     (C)  It makes substantive recommendations which are, and over
          an extended period of time have been, regularly approved
          without significant amendment or modification by another
          public official or governmental agency.

                                        Regulation 18700(a)(1).

     It does not appear that RSBAC will be making any final
governmental decisions.  RSBAC will make recommendations to the
ARB and DF&A which will jointly report to the legislature.  It
does not appear that the ARB and DF&A are bound by any
recommendations made by RSBAC.  Therefore, it appears that the
members of the RSBAC serve a solely advisory function.  However,
if it turns out that the recommendations of RSBAC over an extended
period of time are regularly approved without significant
amendment or modification, it may be necessary for RSBAC to adopt
a conflict of interest code.

     At this time the disclosure provisions of the Act do not
apply to members of the RSBAC.

Our File No.
A-93-087
Page 4


If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Jeff Marschner
General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberl
Political Reform Consultant

A—93—087
